message:
test(fcp-core): pin supply-chain build-environment serde + validation Display (flywheel_connectors-ic6mz)

Bead asks for BuildEnvironment Display formatting. fcp-core has no
type literally named BuildEnvironment; the build-environment cluster
lives in supply_chain.rs as AttestationPredicateType (line 118),
AttestationMaterial (line 128), AttestationMetadata (line 150), and
TrustRootBinding (line 184). None implements Display, so the bead's
"Display formatting" ask has no direct analogue — pinning targets
the serde wire form (predicate URIs surface in attestation logs)
and SupplyChainError Display output (operator-facing audit signal).

Coverage:
- AttestationPredicateType per-variant JSON form pinned to canonical
  predicate URIs ("https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1",
  "https://in-toto.io/Statement/v1") with regression-loud assertion
  since drift silently invalidates every existing attestation
- JSON + CBOR round-trip per variant
- Unknown / wrong-version / wrong-scheme / PascalCase URIs rejected
- AttestationMaterial JSON + CBOR round-trip preserves uri + digest
- AttestationMetadata JSON round-trip preserves all 3 fields;
  invocation_id omitted from wire form when None (per
  skip_serializing_if), present when Some
- AttestationMetadata build timestamps serialize as RFC 3339 UTC
  (chrono default) — pinned for cross-language consumers
- TrustRootBinding JSON round-trip preserves fields for all 3
  documented root_types (sigstore / tuf / manual); root_type
  serialized verbatim (no rename)
- SupplyChainError Display variants surface the reason string for
  operators: InvalidAttestation includes the failing field path,
  InvalidTrustRoot lists the documented root_type set + rejected
  value, InvalidSignature names the required algorithm
- Cross-format consistency: AttestationMaterial/AttestationMetadata/
  AttestationPredicateType round-trip identically through JSON
  and CBOR

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
